name: Fudge a La Louise
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Butter, plus extra for greasing platters
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, mix together the sugar, dark baking chocolate, and milk.
2. Place the pan over high heat and stir constantly until the chocolate is melted and the sugar is dissolved.
3. Bring to a full boil and then lower the heat so the candy continues to boil gently, not vigorously. Stir no more.
4. Put a candy thermometer into the center of the mixture and cook until the temperature reaches exactly 232°F.
5. Meanwhile, butter a large platter (turkey size) and a flat pan about 11 by 13 inches.
6. When the fudge reaches 232°F, pour it into the ready platter without scraping the pan; let it drip out.
7. Dot with 2 tablespoons of butter and let the mixture cool until the platter feels cool underneath.
8. Add vanilla extract.
9. Using a large slotted spoon, start to stir the liquid mixture. It will take about 15 or 20 minutes. You will see a steady change from dark to light color, from glossy to dull, from liquid to solid.
10. When the fudge begins to get dull, add the chopped nuts and mix thoroughly.
11. Put the fudge into the large buttered pan and press into shape with the flat of your palms.
12. Cut into squares; store in an airtight container if there is any left.
